rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS PETER HARVEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.5 Against Maria Elena Benavente And Johnston, Kinney And Zulaica Llp For Frivolous Action
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2 (128.5)] The Harvey sons seek a monetary sanction on this motion, for attorney's fees in the amount of $250,000. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 128.5(2)(A), "Monetary sanctions may not be awarded against a represented party for a violation of presenting a claim, defense, and other legal contentions that are warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment of new law." On the current record, the court does not find that the petitions filed by Benavente were "totally and completely without merit or for the sole purpose of harassing an opposing party." Benavente, through counsel, appears to be "presenting a claim, defense, and other legal contentions that are warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment of new law." Movant's have carefully parsed the allegations of Benavente, and have marshalled evidence, admissions, and legal authorities to defeat her claims. Movants' ability to shine a light on all perceived defects in Benavente's petitions does not render them "totally and completely without merit." Moreover, the Court cannot conclude, on this record, that Benevente's "sole purpose" is to harass; her primary purpose appears to be to receive a larger share of the estate, and pursue the role of executor. The court has not made any legal or factual determinations about the validity or veracity of any legal theory or allegation presented in these competing and opposed petitions. The court declines to make a determination of validity or veracity at this time, on this record, through this motion practice. The pending petitions - and their oppositions - will be heard and adjudicated in the regular process. For the reasons stated above, the Motion for sanctions under section 128.5 is denied. Petitioner Benavente is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2 (1285.)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS PETER HARVEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 Against Johnston, Kinney And Zulaica Llp For Frivolous Action
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2 (128.5)] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Petitioners Thomas Harvey, Peter Harvey, and Douglas Harvey's Motion for Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 128.5 and Against Maria Elena Benavente and Johnson, Kinney and Zulaica LLP for Frivolous Action. Per the Notice of Motion, this motion is brought on the grounds that Benavente, represented by Johnson, Kinney and Zulaica LLP, have filed (1) Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary; (2) Maria Elena Benavente's Objection to Petition for Administration with Will Annexed and Appointment for Administrator with Will Annexed Bennett Hong; (3) Petition for Interpretation of Will, or in the Alternative, Request for Reformation of Will were filed in bad faith, are frivolous and solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. The court GRANTS Petitioners' request for judicial notice. The court GRANTS Petitioner Maria Elena Benavente's request for judicial notice. On its own motion, the court takes judicial notice of the Joint Case Management Statement filed in this case on December 5, 2022. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) The Motion for Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 128.5 and Against Maria Elena Benavente and Johnson, Kinney and Zulaica LLP for Frivolous Action is DENIED. Under CCP 128.5: (a) A trial court may order a party, the party's attorney, or both, to pay the reasonable expenses, including attorney's fees, incurred by another party as a result of actions or tactics, made in bad faith, that are frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. (b) For purposes of this section: [] (2) "Frivolous" means totally and completely without merit or for the sole purpose of harassing an opposing party. This case is approximately six months old. Benavente filed the initial petition for probate on June 28, 2022. The Harvey sons filed a competing petition for probate on July 8, 2022. Benavente filed Petition for Interpretation of Will, or in the Alterative, Request for Reformation of Will, on October 11, 2022. The parties have filed oppositions to each other's petitions for probate. The parties' competing arguments have remained essentially consistent, with minor changes to the factual allegations: Benevente seeks to probate the handwritten materials (which are contended to be a holographic will) and statutory will document at issue as a unified expression of decedent's testamentary intent, under an integration theory. The Harvey sons seek admission of the statutory will document alone as decedent's final and controlling testamentary instrument, contending that the revocation clause in it revoked any prior holograph. [Part 1 of 2 (128.5)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS PETER HARVEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 Against Johnston, Kinney And Zulaica Llp For Frivolous Action (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2 (128.7)] The Motion for Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 Against Johnson, Kinney and Zulaica LLP for Frivolous Action is DENIED. On the current record, the court does not find that the petitions the Harvey sons present as grounds for this motion are "presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay or needless increase in the cost of litigation," or that the "the claims, defenses, and other legal contentions therein are [not] warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment of new law," or that the "allegations and other factual contentions [lack] evidentiary support or, if specifically so identified, are [not] likely to have evidentiary support after a reasonable opportunity for further investigation or discovery, or that the "denials of factual contentions are [not] warranted on the evidence or, if specifically so identified, are [not] reasonably based on a lack of information or belief." This case is approximately six months old. Benavente filed the initial petition for probate on June 28, 2022. The Harvey sons filed a competing petition for probate on July 8, 2022. Benavente filed Petition for Interpretation of Will, on in the Alterative, Request for Reformation of Will, on October 11, 2022. The parties have filed oppositions to each other's petitions for probate. The Harvey sons' opposition to the Petition for Interpretation of Will, etc. has been submitted for filing as of publication of this tentative ruling. Each of the two petitions and the opposition the Harvey sons offer here as grounds for sanctions are verified by Benavente under penalty of perjury. The filings appear to be "presenting a claim, defense, and other legal contentions that are warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment of new law." The court declines to make a determination of validity or veracity at this time, on this record, through this motion practice. The pending petitions - and their oppositions - will be heard and adjudicated in the regular process. For the reasons stated above, the Motion for sanctions under section 128.5 is denied. Petitioner Benavente is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with all substantive provisions of this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2 (128.7)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS PETER HARVEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion For Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.5 Against Maria Elena Benavente And Johnston, Kinney And Zulaica Llp For Frivolous Action (Added For Posting Of The Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2 (128.7)] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Petitioners Thomas Harvey, Peter Harvey, and Douglas Harvey's Motion for Order Imposing Sanctions Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 128.7 Against Johnson, Kinney and Zulaica LLP for Frivolous Action. Per the Notice of Motion, this motion is brought on the grounds that the law firm Johnson, Kinney and Zulaica LLP, in their representation of Maria Elena Benavente in this matter, has filed the following documents without evidentiary support and without being warranted under existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment or new law, in violation of Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 subsection (b): (1) Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary; (2) Maria Elena Benavente's Objection to Petition for Administration with Will Annexed and Appointment for Administrator with Will Annexed Bennett Hong; (3) Petition for Interpretation of Will, or in the Alternative, Request for Reformation of Will. The court GRANTS Petitioners' request for judicial notice. The court GRANTS Petitioner Maria Elena Benavente's request for judicial notice. On its own motion, the court takes judicial notice of the Joint Case Management Statement filed in this case on December 5, 2022. Judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Under Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7(b), By presenting to the court, whether by signing, filing, submitting, or later advocating, a pleading, petition, written notice of motion, or other similar paper, an attorney or unrepresented party is certifying that to the best of the person's knowledge, information, and belief, formed after an inquiry reasonable under the circumstances, all of the following conditions are met: (1) It is not being presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay or needless increase in the cost of litigation. (2) The claims, defenses, and other legal contentions therein are warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment of new law. (3) The allegations and other factual contentions have evidentiary support or, if specifically so identified, are likely to have evidentiary support after a reasonable opportunity for further investigation or discovery. (4) The denials of factual contentions are warranted on the evidence or, if specifically so identified, are reasonably based on a lack of information or belief. Under subsection (c) of the statute, if the court "determines that subdivision (b) has been violated, the court may, subject to the conditions stated below, impose an appropriate sanction upon the attorneys, law firms, or parties that have violated subdivision (b) or are responsible for the violation. In determining what sanctions, if any, should be ordered, the court shall consider whether a party seeking sanctions has exercised due diligence." [Part 1 of 2 (128.7)]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SACHIKO KJERBO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/22 for an appearance. 1) Probate Code Section 6124 provides that, "if the testator's will was last in the testator's possession, the testator was competent until death, and neither the will nor a duplicate original of the will can be found after the testator's death, it is presumed the testator destroyed the will with intent to revoke it. This presumption is a presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence." 2) No evidence has been submitted to overcome the presumption of revocation. A person's attorney may sign or verify a petition, objection or response, if the petitioner, objector or respondent is unable to do so, unless the person is, or is seeking to become, a fiduciary appointed in the proceeding. [CRC 7.103(c).]
